chapters.title: Releasing memory
chapters.description: He speaks about a present that is easier to inhabit when the past and future are no longer pressing for analysis.

chapters.title: Care and rest
chapters.description: The closing reflection asks for cycles of love, devotion, and rest rather than constant caregiving strain.

title: Guided Meditation: If you're looking, you're lost
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins by inviting a felt sense of inspiration and a dharma longing that is different from craving to get somewhere. From there, Matthew Brensilver repeatedly points toward attuning to what serves, and toward the simple recognition that awakening is already available in ordinary experience. The instruction then turns to relinquishing preference, loosening willfulness, and resting in awareness without trying to manage it.
short_summary: A guided meditation on inspiration, non-striving, and resting in the awareness that is already here.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The meditation opens by welcoming the group and inviting a shared settling of body and mind.

chapters.title: Finding Inspiration
chapters.description: He suggests beginning with something genuinely inspiring that evokes longing for the Dharma.

chapters.title: Longing, Not Craving
chapters.description: The talk distinguishes dharma longing from the craving to get somewhere, then turns toward where attention is naturally drawn.

chapters.title: What Serves
chapters.description: Listeners are asked to notice the aperture of attention and choose an anchor in a way that fits the mind's present conditions.

chapters.title: Don't Go Looking
chapters.description: The instruction becomes more direct: don't look outside or even inside for something to find, because looking itself can obscure what is already present.

chapters.title: Radical Inclusion
chapters.description: Brensilver invites relinquishing the preference for pleasure over pain and widening the range of what can be held in practice.

chapters.title: Letting Go of Willfulness
chapters.description: He addresses the fear that goodness will be lost without effort, and gently questions the sense that more doing is still needed.

chapters.title: Awareness Needs No Job
chapters.description: The meditation closes by pointing out that awareness does not need to be propped up, and that the letting-go of effort can feel both sad and relieving.

title: Guided Meditation: Letting go of limitation
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that moves from settling and grounding into a gentle inquiry into how ideas of limitation constrict the mind. He invites listeners to soften self-protective habits, feel safe enough to rest in breath and body, and notice how spaciousness can arise without effort. The talk later widens into equanimity and a quiet recognition that, when experience is no longer so compelling, it becomes more natural to lay burdens down.
short_summary: A guided meditation on releasing self-limiting ideas and allowing spaciousness, safety, and ease to emerge.

chapters.title: Grounding and Settling
chapters.description: The meditation begins by stabilizing attention and finding something in experience that supports settling.

chapters.title: Putting Down Limitation
chapters.description: Matthew opens an inquiry into what it means to release ideas of limitation and the sense of a small, defended self.

chapters.title: Clinging and Vastness
chapters.description: He points to clinging as self-absorbing and suggests that loosening those habits can reveal space and a wider mind.

chapters.title: Safety in the Body
chapters.description: The practice turns toward enough inner safety to stop reiterating oneself and to melt into breath and body.

chapters.title: No Preference
chapters.description: Equanimity is framed as not preferring expansive states over contractive ones, which brings more space and stability.

chapters.title: Nothing to Manufacture
chapters.description: He emphasizes that this opening cannot be forced and leaves it to conditions and natural unfolding.

chapters.title: Samsara and Weariness
chapters.description: The talk later reflects on samsara as enchanting but eventually less compelling, where boredom can support letting go without aversion.

chapters.title: Closing Blessing
chapters.description: The meditation closes with a brief repeated blessing.

title: Guided Meditation: Grounding and Compassion
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins by easing attention into silence and sound, then treats thoughts as just more sound rather than something to cling to. From there, Matthew Brensilver moves into settling, using a Thich Nhat Hanh image of nectar sinking in a glass, and then turns toward compassion by reflecting on suffering, precariousness, and gratitude. The practice becomes more intimate as he invites listeners to bring to mind someone who is suffering, feel the weight of their pain, and let that meeting soften into warmth and care.
short_summary: A guided meditation that moves from settling into silence to compassionate reflection on suffering and love.

chapters.title: Entering Silence
chapters.description: The meditation opens by resting in silence and noticing the play of noise and quiet around attention.

chapters.title: Thoughts as Sound
chapters.description: Matthew invites a relaxed relationship to thinking, treating mental activity as sound and noticing the quiet between thoughts.

chapters.title: Settling Like Nectar
chapters.description: Using Thich Nhat Hanh’s image of nectar settling in juice, he points to a deeper settling that may be true even when it does not feel pleasant.

chapters.title: Suffering and Gratitude
chapters.description: The practice turns toward the truth of suffering, the limits of what can be alleviated, and the gratitude that comes with recognizing precariousness.

chapters.title: Bringing Someone to Mind
chapters.description: Listeners are invited to picture a person who is suffering and to make their pain vivid and specific in the heart.

chapters.title: Taking It In
chapters.description: Matthew names the distress that can arise when we recognize another’s suffering and asks for a willingness to stay open to it.

chapters.title: Love in Response
chapters.description: The teaching shifts from empathic distress toward an expansive response of love and the wish for freedom from suffering.

chapters.title: Softening Into Compassion
chapters.description: The meditation lingers in repeated phrases as heaviness softens into warmth, with the final section marked by extended repetition that is partly unclear in the transcript.

title: Guided Meditation: Forgive all phenomena
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation that begins with settling the body and taking up a careful, relaxed posture. He then turns to the felt mechanics of craving and the habit of always reaching for the next thing, before shifting into the central instruction to “forgive all phenomena” — including pleasantness and pain — and trust the quieting that follows. The talk closes with a more spacious, effortless resting and a few bell tones.
short_summary: A guided meditation on settling the body, seeing the mind’s craving for “next,” and relaxing into forgiveness of all experience.

chapters.title: Opening Into Practice
chapters.description: A brief welcome and invitation to begin the meditation together.

chapters.title: Settling the Body
chapters.description: He guides a gentle way of arriving, taking time with posture and easing into practice without force.

chapters.title: Bored With Samsara
chapters.description: The talk reflects on weariness with praise, gain, and the effort to keep everything together.

chapters.title: The Pull of Next
chapters.description: Craving is described as the repeated movement toward the next thing, even when dressed up as progress.

chapters.title: Just This Breath
chapters.description: Attention narrows to simple awareness of the breath and the immediacy of this moment.

chapters.title: A Few Conscious Breaths
chapters.description: He emphasizes how quickly the mind can shift, even after only a few mindful breaths.

chapters.title: Forgive All Phenomena
chapters.description: The core instruction is to forgive every experience, including both pleasure and pain, and then let go of extra effort.

chapters.title: Resting In Ease
chapters.description: The meditation closes in a quieter, more open resting, ending with bell sounds.

title: Guided Meditation: Meditating on June 19
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation moves gently between breath, body, and awareness, then opens into a quiet reflection on love, freedom, and nonviolence. Matthew Brensilver names Juneteenth and uses it to frame the practice as staying close to love, feeling the intensity of being human, and sensing the innocence of the wish for peace. Later, he points to the image of peace at every sense door—body, eye, ear, and mind—as a way of understanding awakening as a soft, loving openness.
short_summary: A guided meditation on resting with breath, body, and awareness, framed by Juneteenth, love, and peace at every sense door.

chapters.title: Finding the Anchor
chapters.description: The guidance begins by exploring different possible anchors, from the whole field of awareness to the breath and the felt body.

chapters.title: Staying with Awareness
chapters.description: The instruction settles into repeated attention to awareness itself and the way the mind colors each moment.

chapters.title: Juneteenth and Freedom
chapters.description: Matthew names Juneteenth and reflects on cruelty, freedom, and the need to remain close to love.

chapters.title: Peace at the Senses
chapters.description: He draws on a traditional Buddhist description of enlightenment as peace at the body, eye, ear, and mind doors.

chapters.title: Stopping the Fight
chapters.description: The practice turns toward a felt release from self-conflict and conflict with the world, toward radical nonviolence.

chapters.title: Settling in Silence
chapters.description: A long stretch of quiet holding follows, giving space for the practice to deepen without much verbal instruction.

title: Guided Meditation: Following Interest
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins with a Thich Nhat Hanh image of settling, then turns toward letting experience rise rather than forcing it down. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the ease of not having to manage attention, inviting trust that practice can happen without strain or performance. The guidance then shifts into following what is genuinely interesting, treating curiosity as a kind of receptive, non-extractive attention.
short_summary: A gentle guided meditation on settling, trusting practice, and following interest without forcing attention.

chapters.title: Settling Like Nectar
chapters.description: An opening image from Thich Nhat Hanh frames meditation as a settling rather than a struggle.

chapters.title: Letting Things Surface
chapters.description: The guidance describes how less suppression allows what is hidden or active to rise on its own.

chapters.title: Trusting Non-Doing
chapters.description: Matthew challenges the urge to control attention and emphasizes that nothing needs to be manufactured.

chapters.title: Following Interest
chapters.description: The practice turns toward the thread of interest, asking what this moment and this life are.

chapters.title: Curiosity as Practice
chapters.description: Curiosity and investigation are presented as a supportive intention, independent of where attention lands.

chapters.title: Receptive Attention
chapters.description: Attention is reimagined as a blessing rather than a way of extracting something from the moment.

chapters.title: Counting and Closing
chapters.description: The talk moves into extended counting, with the final stretch feeling like a quiet settling out.

title: Guided Meditation: Embodiment and Allowing
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation invites a quiet shift from efforting to willingness, using the breath and body as a way to become more available to experience. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to themes of embodiment, softening, and allowing, especially when meditation feels difficult or impossible. The practice builds toward a spacious view of awareness that can hold pain, aversion, and uncertainty without tightening around them.
short_summary: A guided meditation on embodiment, softening, and allowing experience without forcing the mind.

chapters.title: Opening Intention
chapters.description: The meditation begins by asking what it means to be available to the Dharma and to this moment.

chapters.title: Willingness Over Willfulness
chapters.description: He contrasts a softer willingness with forceful striving and frames effort as alignment rather than control.

chapters.title: Relaxing into Newness
chapters.description: The practice turns toward relaxing more fully and meeting each moment as a fresh beginning.

chapters.title: Body as Refuge
chapters.description: Mindfulness of the body is emphasized as a way to keep presence steady while sights and sounds arise.

chapters.title: Breath into the Bones
chapters.description: Attention moves deeper into the belly and bones, with an invitation to live the moment less through conceptual thought.

chapters.title: Coping and Openness
chapters.description: He reflects on thinking as a form of coping and asks how much openness awareness can hold.

chapters.title: Softening the Impossible
chapters.description: When practice feels impossible or helpless, the instruction is to soften, keep the heart open, and remain available.

chapters.title: Freedom Without a Sign
chapters.description: The talk closes with images of freedom, emptiness, and loving phenomena so completely that the selfing around them can loosen.

title: Guided Meditation: Fresh Moment
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: In this guided meditation, Matthew Brensilver invites a quiet shift from expecting the next moment to meeting experience freshly. He frames practice around not being startled by impermanence, relaxing what can soften, and letting the breath hold the body with a steady, soothing presence. Later, he turns toward curiosity, not-knowing, and the release of self-repetition as ways to stay awake and alive to what is here.
short_summary: A guided meditation on impermanence, curiosity, and meeting each moment without expectation.

chapters.title: Arriving and settling
chapters.description: A brief opening welcomes listeners and turns attention toward posture and beginning the practice together.

chapters.title: Not startled by change
chapters.description: The meditation frames practice as learning to meet impermanence without being jolted by it, softening resistance to the changing nature of experience.

chapters.title: Breath as soothing support
chapters.description: Attention turns to the felt experience of breathing as something gently holding and caressing the body.

chapters.title: Allowing the present moment
chapters.description: He encourages saying yes to the imperfections of this moment without making that a permanent endorsement, and describes each breath as a way of becoming more secluded and present.

chapters.title: Following genuine interest
chapters.description: When curiosity arises, he suggests treating it as a trustworthy thread for practice rather than trying to manufacture interest.

chapters.title: The aliveness of not knowing
chapters.description: The talk opens into the poetry of noticing familiar experience in slightly new ways and staying close to uncertainty without closing it down.

chapters.title: Releasing expectation
chapters.description: He invites listeners to put down expectations of finding themselves in the next moment.

chapters.title: Quiet and selfing
chapters.description: The meditation closes by asking what it means to cease reiterating the self, grounding that inquiry in breath, body, and quiet.

title: Guided Meditation: dropping secret agendas (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on loosening hidden striving: the wish to find, fix, control, or curate experience. He moves from relaxing the body and letting the breath, sounds, and awareness arrive on their own, toward a quieter look at how grasping and aversion shape attention and the sense of self. In the final section he turns to walking meditation as a way to carry steadiness, release, and beginning again into daily movement.
short_summary: A guided meditation on releasing secret agendas, softening selfing, and learning steadiness through attention and walking practice.

chapters.title: Settle and relax
chapters.description: The meditation begins with simple permission to relax and not go looking for anything special.

chapters.title: Hidden agendas
chapters.description: Brensilver names the subtle drives beneath practice—wanting to make things happen, make them stay, or control the shape of attention.

chapters.title: Wanting and attention
chapters.description: He describes how unnoticed grasping and aversion sponsor meditation and pull attention into the drama of gain and loss.

chapters.title: Let the self loosen
chapters.description: The talk turns to the meditator role itself, inviting the listener to let down the endless curation of self and receive the body, breath, and soundscape more simply.

chapters.title: Surrendering words
chapters.description: When difficult emotions and old fears arise, he points to the impulse to solve sorrow with words and suggests briefly releasing that habit.

chapters.title: Practice in the hall
chapters.description: The middle of the talk settles into extended guidance for sitting practice, emphasizing receptivity, awareness, and staying with experience as it is.

chapters.title: First and second arrows
chapters.description: Near the end he distinguishes unavoidable pain from the extra suffering added by resistance, while also acknowledging grief that cannot be dissolved.

chapters.title: Walking as training
chapters.description: He closes by explaining walking meditation as training in continuity, letting go at each turn, and carrying calm alertness into ordinary movement.

title: Guided Metta Meditation - Metta for All Beings (Retreat at Spirit Rock)
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver frames metta as one of the Brahma-viharas, or “four species of love,” then shows how loving-kindness can function as cultivation, concentration, and purification. The talk moves from the way metta loosens self-view to a guided practice that begins with the body and phrases, then opens outward to all beings. It ends by holding the sincerity of the wish alongside the fact that suffering is vast and unresolved.
short_summary: A guided metta practice that explores loving-kindness as wisdom, concentration, purification, and a way of releasing self-view.

chapters.title: Four kinds of love
chapters.description: Matthew opens by framing the Brahma-viharas as different ways love meets goodness, suffering, success, and helplessness.

chapters.title: Love changes self-view
chapters.description: He uses a retreat story to show how even a brief moment of metta can reorganize the relationship to self and loosen the self-as-project mode.

chapters.title: Measureless and quiet
chapters.description: The talk turns to the immeasurables and the reminder that loving-kindness may be subtle, quiet, and not defined by intensity.

chapters.title: Cultivation and purification
chapters.description: Matthew explains metta as cultivation, concentration, and purification, including the way practice can bring buried grief or rage to the surface.

chapters.title: Settling into practice
chapters.description: The guided section begins with body, posture, and relaxation, then points to worthiness beyond self-judgment and the habit of proving oneself.

chapters.title: Phrases in the present
chapters.description: He introduces the idea of a mind made vast by love and uses simple phrases as a present-moment way to access the heart.

chapters.title: Resting in kindness
chapters.description: The practice widens through the recognition that whatever is arising can be held, whether by quiet rest or by bringing care to what has not yet been loved.

chapters.title: Love in hard places
chapters.description: Matthew names this as non-self-indulgent love and notes how tight spots can reveal the necessity of love that makes hatred less tenable.

chapters.title: All beings at ease
chapters.description: The meditation culminates in the wish for all beings to be safe, protected, and at peace, even while acknowledging that suffering does not end.

title: Guided Meditation: Non-referentiality
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver begins with a light, self-aware basketball anecdote and then turns quickly into a guided meditation on settling, relaxing, and sensing the body as light and gravity. The main teaching explores how the mind locates itself through reference points—body, preference, craving, aversion—and invites a period of practice “without referentiality.” The closing section softens into endings, dissolution, and a Rumi line about living in the nowhere you come from, returning to spaciousness without fixed self-reference.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing into the body and resting without reference points.

chapters.title: Opening and arrival
chapters.description: Matthew opens with a brief basketball story and then invites everyone to settle in for the meditation.

chapters.title: Brightness and body
chapters.description: The practice begins by relaxing everything except brightness, filling the body with light, and letting the center of gravity drop into the belly.

chapters.title: Held by earth
chapters.description: He emphasizes gravity, the earth holding us fast, and fuller breaths through the belly.

chapters.title: Softening more
chapters.description: The instruction becomes simple and repetitive, encouraging a gradual softening into the sitting.

chapters.title: Reference points
chapters.description: Matthew uses the image of a phone pinging towers to explain how we locate ourselves through body, preferences, plans, craving, and aversion.

chapters.title: Without referentiality
chapters.description: The meditation shifts into a long refrain asking what it means to be here without reference points and to lay down sight, sound, sensation, and thought.

chapters.title: Endings and dissolution
chapters.description: Near the end, he invites tolerance for endings, things dissolving, and not needing to pick anything else up.

chapters.title: Nowhere you come from
chapters.description: A Rumi quotation deepens the final spaciousness, and the closing returns again and again to non-identification, including the meditator and the act of teaching itself as just another ping.

title: Guided Medtiation: Checking Hindrances, Checking Defilements
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins with settling into the body and then scans the five hindrances as a practical pulse check: restlessness, dullness, craving, aversion, and doubt. The emphasis stays gentle and observational, with repeated invitations to notice what is present without forcing anything away. As the practice unfolds, the talk turns toward relaxing the effort to control experience and allowing attention to become more open, unmixed, and receptive.
short_summary: A gentle guided meditation that checks the five hindrances and invites a quieter, less grasping kind of attention.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The meditation opens by landing in the body and establishing a steady, receptive ground for practice.

chapters.title: Scanning Hindrances
chapters.description: The teacher introduces the five hindrances and invites a simple check-in with whatever is present.

chapters.title: Bearing Unpleasantness
chapters.description: The focus turns to how unpleasantness is met in the nervous system and how awareness can hold it without judgment.

chapters.title: Choosing an Anchor
chapters.description: Different possible anchors are named, from breath and body to sound and open awareness.

chapters.title: Unmixed Attention
chapters.description: The guidance emphasizes relaxing, receiving experience, and letting attention bow rather than grasp.

chapters.title: Letting Go Of Control
chapters.description: The talk points toward surrendering control and noticing that goodness does not need to be manufactured.

chapters.title: Putting Down Yardsticks
chapters.description: The closing movement gestures toward releasing comparison and measurement, and simply resting with what is here.

title: Guided Meditation: Softening the Self
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on softening the felt sense of self through relaxation, safety, and widening awareness. The practice begins with posture and settling, then turns toward the strain of clinging and willfulness, suggesting that letting go reveals a quieter brightness already present. In the later stages, he explores the self as a knot of tension and invites a more porous, fluid way of being, with an emphasis on anicca and the way identity can loosen when the mind feels safe.
short_summary: A guided practice of relaxing effort, meeting clinging gently, and allowing the sense of self to soften and open.

chapters.title: Arrive and Settle
chapters.description: The meditation opens by welcoming listeners, acknowledging the day's condition, and inviting a settled posture.

chapters.title: Relax into Brightness
chapters.description: He guides a full-body softening while asking the listener to stay tethered to the mind's natural brightness and luminosity.

chapters.title: Held by Love
chapters.description: The practice widens into a sense of shared presence, with repeated reminders to relax even the urge to do more.

chapters.title: Clinging and Letting Go
chapters.description: He names the strain of clinging, striving, and willfulness, then asks what remains when those habits are relaxed.

chapters.title: The Knot of Self
chapters.description: The talk shifts to the self as a knot of tension and explores how safety reduces trance and grasping.

chapters.title: Softening and Widening
chapters.description: Using the image of a snagging hangnail, he repeatedly invites softening and widening until attention feels more spacious.

chapters.title: Impermanence and Porosity
chapters.description: He brings in anicca and asks how deeply impermanence runs, pointing to a self that can melt, become porous, and fluid.

chapters.title: Closing Reflection
chapters.description: The meditation ends with one more invitation to notice how the self may be grasped or may open into something larger.

title: Guided Meditation: Arousal and Valence Signifying Nothing
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on working directly with arousal and valence: how intensity, pleasantness, and unpleasantness are sensed in the body, and how they can be held without turning immediately into stories. The practice emphasizes settling with breath, widening the field of experience, and allowing feeling to remain vivid without making it mean too much. As the talk develops, the instruction turns toward decoupling emotion from elaboration so that experience can be present without collapsing into identity or self-judgment.
short_summary: A guided meditation on meeting bodily feeling with clarity, equanimity, and less identification.

chapters.title: Settling and Soothe
chapters.description: The meditation opens by anchoring to something settled and noticing that intensity can coexist with something soothing in the field of experience.

chapters.title: Pleasant and Unpleasant
chapters.description: He names the difference between subtle pleasant feeling and the stickier pull of pain, worry, and heartache.

chapters.title: Why Feeling Feels Safe
chapters.description: The instructions explore how feeling can seem protective and how the mind treats certain sensations as a basis for safety.

chapters.title: Tracking Arousal
chapters.description: Brensilver defines arousal and valence as body-level phenomena and links them to the felt texture of emotion and craving.

chapters.title: Giving Feeling Space
chapters.description: The practice widens into a larger field where feeling is allowed as much space as it needs, instead of being crowded into a narrow story.

chapters.title: Worth and Unconscious Feeling
chapters.description: He points to how questions of worthiness and value can arise from unconscious feeling and then repeatedly returns to clarity, equanimity, and alertness.

chapters.title: Tranquility Pulls In
chapters.description: A quieter phase develops as tranquility becomes more available and the body is guided to register feeling without forcing interpretation.

chapters.title: Decoupling Thought Loops
chapters.description: The core instruction emerges: when breathing is disturbed, practice separating arousal and valence from automatic loops of thought and letting feeling signify nothing.

chapters.title: Resting in Breath
chapters.description: The meditation settles into resting with whatever is pleasant in the breath, then continuing to stay intimate with breathing as the sense of self softens.

title: Guided Meditation: Surrendering the Territory of Me
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins with simple sensory attention and gradually widens into a softer relationship with body, sound, and awareness. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to the felt sense of self as a territory we patrol, then invites a gentler letting go of that guarding and a more intimate experience of spaciousness. The closing instructions emphasize non-preference: meeting contraction and ease alike without making them a problem.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing the defended sense of self and resting in non-preferential awareness.

chapters.title: Settling on sound
chapters.description: Attention opens with the faintest sound and the edge between hearing and silence.

chapters.title: Soft gaze inward
chapters.description: The practice shifts to resting visual attention in the darkness or brightness behind closed eyes.

chapters.title: Weight and ease
chapters.description: Instructions return again and again to grounded bodily sensation, heaviness, and simple restful alertness.

chapters.title: The guarded territory
chapters.description: Matthew names the sense of self as a protected boundary and invites recognition of the effort to patrol it.

chapters.title: Lay down your arms
chapters.description: The defense of self and world is softened, and the practice turns toward relaxing resistance.

chapters.title: Contracting and opening
chapters.description: He points to self as a contractive force and then to the expansive side where self and not-self are made of the same elements.

chapters.title: Life happening
chapters.description: The meditation broadens into allowing life to be experienced as happening, without centering everything on 'me.'

chapters.title: No preference
chapters.description: The talk turns toward loving preferences and control without taking them seriously, and toward peace that does not depend on phenomena.

chapters.title: Closing reflection
chapters.description: Matthew briefly cites Shinzen Young on non-preference, then ends by inviting an intuitive sense of self as a defended boundary.

title: Guided Meditation: The Pull of Time
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that moves from relaxing tension in the body into a broader reflection on care, attention, and the limits of control. The practice repeatedly returns to kindness toward pain and tightness, then widens into the ways wanting and fear shape attention and keep us oriented toward a future that never fully arrives. In the closing arc, he points to letting go, receiving the present moment, and recognizing that insight into the Four Noble Truths can be available right now.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing tension, softening grasping, and meeting the present moment without control.

chapters.title: Settling and Relaxing
chapters.description: The meditation begins by relaxing whatever can be relaxed and blessing what remains tense to stay as it is.

chapters.title: Tending the Body
chapters.description: Attention turns to bodily tightness or pain, with a suggestion to gently massage discomfort while staying in the flow of practice.

chapters.title: Care and Lineage
chapters.description: He frames self-care as an expression of loving-kindness and places the listener within a long lineage of love and care.

chapters.title: Practice and Human Longing
chapters.description: The talk widens to the wish to be free of suffering, the work of learning on the cushion, and the distinction between what is worthy of protection and what can be released.

chapters.title: Wanting and Fear
chapters.description: Brensilver reflects on how hope gets projected into the future and how renunciation loosens ownership, control, and our relationship to helplessness.

chapters.title: Letting Go to Receive
chapters.description: Using the phrase about not fooling Mother Nature, he emphasizes that we cannot control experience and must let go to receive this moment.

chapters.title: Insight on Offer
chapters.description: The meditation opens into a quieter teaching on recognizing what is happening, with the suggestion that insight can be available in any moment.

chapters.title: Not Missing It
chapters.description: The closing line returns to the simple task of doing our best not to miss the present opportunity.

title: Guided Meditation: Relax and Let go
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation settles first into posture and deeper breathing, then moves toward a quieter willingness to stop trying to solve life. Matthew Brensilver emphasizes surrender, rest, and receiving the breath more fully, with a brief image from Thich Nhat Hanh and a nod to the Three Refuges as sources of soothing. The latter part becomes a long counting practice, steadying attention through repetition and simplicity.
short_summary: A quiet guided meditation on relaxing, surrendering control, and resting in the breath, ending with extended counting practice.

chapters.title: Arriving to meditate
chapters.description: The meditation opens with a brief welcome and a turn into practice.

chapters.title: Posture and breath
chapters.description: Attention settles into the body, posture, and a few deeper breaths.

chapters.title: Letting settle
chapters.description: A Thich Nhat Hanh image supports the sense of the body sinking and settling.

chapters.title: Releasing the urge
chapters.description: Matthew turns toward renouncing the impulse to solve life and the freedom of surrender.

chapters.title: What feels restful
chapters.description: The instruction narrows to whatever is already restful, including body, silence, and refuge.

chapters.title: Resting with practice
chapters.description: The repeated invitation to rest continues, allowing the mood of ease to deepen.

chapters.title: Receiving the breath
chapters.description: The practice shifts back to the breath, including its beginnings and endings and the suggestion to place attention elsewhere if helpful.

chapters.title: Counting practice
chapters.description: The talk moves into a long sustained counting sequence, using repetition to steady awareness and reduce effort.

title: Guided Meditation: Relaxing the tension called 'me'
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation invites a softening around the felt tension of selfing, moving from settling the body into a careful inquiry into how control is maintained through feeling, thought, and image. Matthew Brensilver repeatedly returns to surrender as a simple laying down of strategy, and toward the end he emphasizes relaxing the contraction that accompanies uncertainty. The practice stays quiet and direct, with the instruction to notice what can relax and to allow the rest to remain.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing the contraction of self and releasing the impulse to control experience.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: After a brief welcome, the practice begins by finding posture and arriving in stillness.

chapters.title: Touching Tranquility
chapters.description: He points to the muscle memory of Dharma practice and suggests recollecting tranquility and openheartedness.

chapters.title: Relax What Can
chapters.description: The instruction narrows to relaxing whatever can be relaxed while allowing remaining tension to stay.

chapters.title: Self as Headquarters
chapters.description: The talk turns to the sense of self as a headquarters for control, and surrender as the self laying down.

chapters.title: Seeing the Mechanics
chapters.description: He names the body, mental images, words, and auditory thought as the tools that organize clinging and control.

chapters.title: From Control to Wakefulness
chapters.description: The emphasis shifts from acquiring and strategizing to simply being awake, without a hidden agenda.

chapters.title: Contraction and Uncertainty
chapters.description: Near the end, he asks listeners to notice self-contraction as weight and to relax the uncertainty that fuels control.

chapters.title: Habitualizing Impermanence
chapters.description: The closing line gestures toward habituating to anicca as the practice continues.

title: Guided Meditation: Stillness and the Dissolution of the Predictive Mind
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with settling the body and gradually shifts toward the breath as a simple object of awareness. He links relaxation to a softer relationship with dukkha, then moves into a teaching on how meditation loosens the mind’s habit of predicting the next moment and imagining continuity. The talk closes by pointing toward samadhi as a quiet dissolution of that predictive architecture and a more direct falling into the present.
short_summary: A guided meditation on bodily settling, the breath, and how stillness can dissolve the mind’s habit of predicting the next moment.

chapters.title: Opening and settling in
chapters.description: Matthew greets the group and invites everyone into practice with a quiet, communal tone.

chapters.title: Doing much less
chapters.description: He frames meditation as a response to life feeling overwhelming, a way of doing less and becoming open to experience.

chapters.title: Softening the body
chapters.description: The practice turns to releasing tension in the face, jaw, shoulders, and belly, with repeated reminders to soften and let go.

chapters.title: Grounding and openness
chapters.description: He includes the hips, pelvis, feet, and hands, emphasizing a sense of energy moving through the body without obstruction.

chapters.title: Breath and aliveness
chapters.description: The breath becomes the main object of awareness, and the talk settles into enjoying simplicity and the pleasure of breathing.

chapters.title: Predicting the future
chapters.description: The teaching shifts from bodily calm to the mind’s subtle habit of leaning forward with expectations and projections.

chapters.title: Samadhi and craving
chapters.description: Matthew describes samadhi as a dissolution of predictive architecture and connects craving with the word 'next.'

chapters.title: Falling into presence
chapters.description: He softens the assumption that 'I am next,' encouraging a direct return to the present moment and a release of self-making.

title: Guided Meditation: Much Becomes Incidental
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on listening to silence and letting the usual mental inventory become incidental. The practice repeatedly returns to receiving the moment without project-making, self-management, or the need to solve experience. As the talk unfolds, surrender is framed not as passivity but as a temporary release of control in which even difficulty can be held more lightly.
short_summary: A guided meditation on listening to silence, releasing control, and letting much of ordinary mental life become incidental.

chapters.title: Listening to silence
chapters.description: The meditation begins by inviting attention to silence itself, even when there is noise around it.

chapters.title: What becomes incidental
chapters.description: Pleasant and unpleasant feeling, autobiography, and fixed ideas are allowed to recede for a little while.

chapters.title: Receiving the moment
chapters.description: The teaching settles into the simple stance that receiving the present moment is enough.

chapters.title: No projects, no building
chapters.description: Brensilver emphasizes dropping the impulse to build, manage, or improve experience.

chapters.title: Surrender and love
chapters.description: Surrender is described as the dissolution of volitional energy, with love named as the one thing worth building.

chapters.title: Receiving space
chapters.description: The practice widens into a spacious resting in which space seems to receive space.

chapters.title: A new chance to let go
chapters.description: The talk returns to the ordinary urge to medicate feelings and thought, and frames the session as another opportunity to release control.

chapters.title: Emerging unchanged
chapters.description: The closing notes suggest that life will still be there afterward, the same and yet slightly different after practice.

title: Guided Meditation: Reverence & Renunciation
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that frames practice as both reverence and renunciation. He invites a posture of sanctity without strain, then guides attention to the breath as a way of putting down multitasking, worry, story-making, and clinging. The talk widens into a quiet reflection on lineage, sincerity, and the way letting go can simplify experience and make breathing more intimate.
short_summary: A guided meditation on reverence, renunciation, and meeting this moment with less clinging and more intimacy.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: A brief welcome and opening transition into shared practice.

chapters.title: Posture as Reverence
chapters.description: He frames posture as an open heart toward the world, with sanctity and relaxation held together.

chapters.title: Breath and Softening
chapters.description: The practice moves into the belly breath, encouraging a fuller and slower rhythm.

chapters.title: Sacred Lineage
chapters.description: He reminds listeners that even familiar meditation is part of a larger lineage of wisdom and compassion.

chapters.title: What to Put Down
chapters.description: Renunciation is defined as a simple, non-moralistic recognition of what can be set aside for now.

chapters.title: Story and Hope
chapters.description: He points to the tendency to proliferate around sense contact and to postpone life into the future.

chapters.title: Clinging Simplifies
chapters.description: The talk turns to the false promises of clinging and the relief that comes when grasping is released.

chapters.title: Resolution and Bells
chapters.description: He names samadhi as a momentary resolution of ambivalence, then the meditation closes in silence with bells.

title: Guided Meditation: All Phenomena are Friendly
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation that steadily loosens the pressure of self-judgment and future-minded striving. He invites listeners to set down the habit of making the self into an object, rest in the breath, and become less intimidated by uncertainty. The practice gradually opens into a quieter Dharma perspective: experience can be met more softly, even when it hurts.
short_summary: A guided meditation on relaxing self-pressure, resting in breath, and meeting experience with the view that all phenomena are friendly.

chapters.title: Arrival and settling
chapters.description: A brief welcome and orientation into practice, with an invitation to find a posture that balances alertness and relaxation.

chapters.title: Letting go of selfing
chapters.description: The meditation turns toward dropping the habit of assessing oneself and forgetting familiar stories about who we are.

chapters.title: Breath and fresh space
chapters.description: Guidance opens into breathing and bodily ease, encouraging a simpler relationship to the present moment.

chapters.title: Pressure to perform
chapters.description: Matthew explores how self-concern accumulates and how relaxing can soften the drive to prove, perform, or become.

chapters.title: Uncertainty and release
chapters.description: The talk names anicca and the burden of trying to manage every future self, then encourages resting in the open space of this breath.

chapters.title: Precision without tension
chapters.description: The meditation becomes more specific about the breath, with attention to beginnings, endings, and the pause of release.

chapters.title: All phenomena friendly
chapters.description: The central phrase is repeated and then clarified as a Dharma view: everything can be met as friendly, even when some experiences hurt.

title: Guided Meditation: Arriving and Opening
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a gentle guided meditation centered on arriving, softening self-conscious effort, and letting the body be as it is. The practice moves from settling posture and attention, to checking for places that need patience or care, to resting with the breath and the felt sense of the body. He closes by framing the practice in terms of relinquishment, true nourishment, and a quiet metta-like response to agitation.
short_summary: A quiet guided meditation on arriving, softening, and finding ease through relinquishment.

chapters.title: Arriving Gently
chapters.description: He begins by encouraging an unhurried arrival, without forcing posture or shaping attention.

chapters.title: Sensing What Needs Care
chapters.description: The practice widens to notice anything in the body or mind that may need awareness, patience, or softening.

chapters.title: Relaxing Self-Image
chapters.description: He points toward body sensations that hurt, and invites a release of self-conscious tension and fixed ideas about the body.

chapters.title: Breathing With Safety
chapters.description: The guidance settles on breathing with whatever alarm or resistance arises, staying close to the beginning and ending of each breath.

chapters.title: Relinquishment and Nourishment
chapters.description: He turns to the Buddha's teaching that peace comes through relinquishment and Ajahn Chah's image of 'food for the heart.'

chapters.title: Extended Silent Practice
chapters.description: A long stretch of quiet practice follows, with the meditation continuing in silence.

chapters.title: Meeting Agitation
chapters.description: He returns to naming restlessness, finding something stable, and responding to ill-will with a brief, direct metta.

title: Guided Meditation: Mostly Quiet Sit
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This mostly quiet guided sit begins with a simple invitation to stop fighting awareness and rest with what cannot be stopped. It then widens into several possible anchors—breath, body, soundscape, feeling tone, and even auditory thinking—while repeatedly softening the sense of effort and self-management. In the last portion, Matthew turns toward a poetic line about a burden that remains heavy even when understood as empty, framing practice as careful attention and love rather than dismissal.
short_summary: A quiet guided meditation on resting with awareness, softening effort, and including sounds, thoughts, and difficulty with care.

chapters.title: Rest with awareness
chapters.description: The meditation opens by asking listeners to stop fighting awareness and rest with what remains present.

chapters.title: Choose an anchor
chapters.description: He offers several possible anchors, including breath, body, soundscape, and hedonic tone.

chapters.title: Ease the selfing
chapters.description: The practice turns toward noticing how self-identified effort, pressure, and patrol can accumulate in the field of awareness.

chapters.title: Soften, widen, relax
chapters.description: A long, repeated period of instruction settles into simple cues to soften, widen, and relax without pushing experience.

chapters.title: Include inner sound
chapters.description: He suggests listening to auditory thinking as sound, without making it special or pushing it away.

chapters.title: Heavy becomes light
chapters.description: The closing teaching uses a poetic line about burden, emptiness, and love to frame difficult experience as something to be held carefully.

title: Guided Meditation: Life without Words
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation centered on the question of what life feels like without words. The practice moves from noticing the mind’s urge to narrativize experience, through working with urgency and craving, into a long stretch of silence and a gradual turn toward stillness. Near the end, he explicitly frames the meditation in terms of relinquishing ownership and self-making, invoking the Buddha’s instruction to give up what is not yours.
short_summary: A guided meditation that softens narration, meets urgency, and opens into stillness and not-self.

chapters.title: Opening question
chapters.description: The meditation begins by asking what life would feel like without words, setting the tone of inquiry and nonverbal attention.

chapters.title: Meaning and narration
chapters.description: He reflects on how words make meaning, and how practice can loosen the compulsion to turn each moment into a story.

chapters.title: Sense experience
chapters.description: Attention is guided toward raw sense experience and the habit of checking what is okay and not okay.

chapters.title: Fewer words
chapters.description: The instruction narrows toward breathing and repeated reminders to use fewer and fewer words.

chapters.title: Working with urgency
chapters.description: A pool of feeling and urgency is acknowledged, with an invitation to listen without being overtaken by it.

chapters.title: Silence pervades
chapters.description: A long silent stretch deepens the practice, including the reminder that nothing needs to be acquired, proved, or become anyone.

chapters.title: Give up ownership
chapters.description: The Buddha reference to giving up what is not yours opens into a repeated inquiry about ownership, answered with 'everything.'

chapters.title: Stillness as object
chapters.description: The practice turns to stillness directly, with repeated instructions to take stillness as the object and not go anywhere.

chapters.title: Closing and transition
chapters.description: The meditation ends by easing out of formal practice while staying with the body, the visual field, and a non-grasping attitude.

title: Guided Meditation: Being Held, Settling In
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with the felt sense of being held by the world, using rain, earth, and bodily support as doorways into settling. The practice moves through breath awareness, working with urgency and false alarms, and then broadens into noticing suffering more clearly without being overwhelmed by it. In the second half, he introduces walking meditation, alternating anchors like seeing, hearing, feet, whole body, and loving-kindness, and closes by emphasizing freshness, renunciation, and trust in the Dharma.
short_summary: A guided meditation on being supported, settling with the breath, meeting urgency and suffering, and then shifting into walking practice with flexible attentional anchors.

chapters.title: Feeling Held
chapters.description: He begins with rain and the sense of being held by the world, inviting a bodily experience of support.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The instruction turns toward making peace with imperfection and resting in what is subtly pleasant.

chapters.title: Letting Go Of Time
chapters.description: He asks listeners to release orientation to retreat days and fall more fully into the present.

chapters.title: Breath Cycle
chapters.description: Attention is gathered on the breath, including the beginning and end of inhalation and exhalation and the brief release at the bottom.

chapters.title: False Alarms
chapters.description: He names the flood of signals in body and mind and suggests treating phenomena as false alarms rather than commands.

chapters.title: Working With Urgency
chapters.description: A whirlpool of urgency is met with recognition, blessing, and breathing until the pull loosens and breathing is available again.

chapters.title: Suffering As Dukkha
chapters.description: If suffering arises, he reframes the moment as an occasion to realize dukkha more clearly.

chapters.title: Walking Meditation
chapters.description: The talk shifts to walking practice, explaining posture, open eyes, visual stimulation, boredom, and the quiet accrual of goodness.

chapters.title: Fresh Anchors
chapters.description: He closes by encouraging variation in the walking anchor, creative freshness, and a final trust in the logic of Dharma.

title: Guided Meditation: Peace is Close
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation centered on settling the body, setting worries aside without force, and softening into immediate experience. The practice moves from simple breathing to a felt sense of defenselessness, then to noticing how the mind elaborates and constructs a self. In the later portion, the teaching opens into a subtle contemplation of being unseen, empty, and no longer held together by familiar identity.
short_summary: A gentle guided meditation on settling down, breathing simply, and loosening the sense of self.

chapters.title: Arriving Together
chapters.description: He welcomes the group and invites them to practice without needing to explain much.

chapters.title: Landing In Breath
chapters.description: The meditation begins with deeper breaths and a simple settling into the body.

chapters.title: Putting Worries Down
chapters.description: He asks listeners to make room for worries without abandoning them, and to stand defenseless before the moment.

chapters.title: Offering The Heart
chapters.description: The instruction becomes repeated and plain: lay down life-projects and offer the heart to what is here.

chapters.title: Breathing Without Elaboration
chapters.description: He explores how experience gets elaborated and points back to the simplicity of just being a body breathing.

chapters.title: Small Shifts Matter
chapters.description: He acknowledges how hard practice can feel, then points to subtle, nearby shifts that can be noticed and appreciated.

chapters.title: Constructing Self
chapters.description: The talk turns to how selfing is built from quiet assumptions and unnoticed sense data.

chapters.title: Unseen And Empty
chapters.description: He invites a contemplation of never being seen as an object and closes with the image of empty phenomena rolling on.

title: Guided Meditation: Sangha & Self
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on Sangha and self, beginning with a brief reflection on why community matters for Dharma practice. He draws on Agnes Callard’s Open Socrates to frame wisdom as something we arrive at with others, then leads the group into a meditation on mutual dependence, self-view, and the subtle ways identity organizes experience. The practice opens toward noticing how the sense of “I” is built, and how that can soften when seen clearly.
short_summary: A guided meditation on Sangha, mutual dependence, and how seeing self-view more clearly can loosen the center of “I.”

chapters.title: Opening and context
chapters.description: Matthew greets the group, responds briefly to a question about substitute teachers, and explains why the evening will focus on community and practice together.

chapters.title: Open Socrates
chapters.description: He introduces Agnes Callard’s Open Socrates and uses its argument about needing others to think well as a bridge into Dharma practice.

chapters.title: The jewel of Sangha
chapters.description: He names Sangha as a true jewel, equal to Buddha and Dharma, and invites the group to feel the goodness of collective practice before settling in.

chapters.title: Mutual dependence
chapters.description: The meditation begins with breath and goodwill, emphasizing that we cannot wake up alone and that we stand on one another’s shoulders.

chapters.title: Seeing self-view
chapters.description: The teaching turns toward self-models, self-view, and the way another person can help reveal assumptions that are usually hidden.

chapters.title: Noticing the vantage point
chapters.description: Matthew points to the ways experience is organized around a presumed vantage point and encourages dis-identifying from it through careful noticing.

chapters.title: Subtle images and words
chapters.description: The close narrows in on the subtle feeling, words, and images that become the center of gravity of 'I am-ness' when unnoticed, and empty when seen.

title: Guided Meditation: Abiding in Goodness
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on abiding in goodness, beginning with the Dalai Lama’s claim that kindness and compassion are necessities rather than luxuries. The practice moves from recollecting love as nourishment, to sensing the ethical atmosphere of sangha, to widening into the shared wish of all beings to be well. Later he brings in the Buddha and Joan Halifax to connect self-care, non-harming, and the embodied balance of a "strong back, soft front."
short_summary: A guided meditation on resting in love, widening compassion, and meeting goodness with both tenderness and resilience.

chapters.title: Love as Necessity
chapters.description: The talk opens by asking what it means to treat kindness and compassion as essential nourishment rather than optional comfort.

chapters.title: Abiding in Love
chapters.description: Matthew turns toward sangha and invites listeners to settle into an image, phrase, or felt sense of love and remain there.

chapters.title: Self and Others
chapters.description: The practice widens into the recognition that every movement of mind and body is a search for happiness, leading into compassion for oneself and others.

chapters.title: Grief and Courage
chapters.description: He notes that contact with goodness can feel like grief or ecstasy, then introduces Joan Halifax’s "strong back, soft front" as an embodied balance.

chapters.title: Deep Faith in Love
chapters.description: The meditation settles into the sense that love may feel tender or may simply allow whatever needs to be felt, both expressing confidence in love.

chapters.title: Closing Refrain
chapters.description: He repeats the body-based image of softness and strength, ending with the reminder that love may feel like love or may simply make room for experience.

title: Guided Meditation: Freeing Experience
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation built around a simple reversal: freedom is not getting a self to win, but loosening the tendency to own experience. He begins with posture and breath, then gradually widens the field to include sensations, feelings, thoughts, and the sense of being a protagonist. The practice settles into a quieter intimacy with awareness, where experience can arise and pass without being seized.
short_summary: A guided meditation on freeing experience by relaxing ownership and allowing awareness to include more of life.

chapters.title: Freedom Reframed
chapters.description: The talk opens with Gil Fronsdal’s line and a Majjhima Nikaya reference to frame liberation as freeing experience rather than securing a self.

chapters.title: Settling the Body
chapters.description: He guides posture, lengthening the spine, grounding into the earth, and arriving with the breath.

chapters.title: Including What Appears
chapters.description: The practice expands into letting experience take its place in awareness without being pushed away or caught.

chapters.title: Freeing Experience
chapters.description: He names images, sounds, pain, tranquility, feelings, and thoughts as things to be freed rather than possessed.

chapters.title: Less Possession
chapters.description: The meditation turns toward the strain of possessiveness and the release of the sense that experience must be held as mine.

chapters.title: Impersonal Arising
chapters.description: Arising and passing come to feel less personal, with awareness taking the foreground and continuity of self loosening.

chapters.title: Equanimity and Control
chapters.description: He reflects on control, delusion, and how equanimity lets the self thin out.

chapters.title: Resting in Awareness
chapters.description: The final minutes are mostly silence and bell chimes, allowing the meditation to settle without further instruction.

title: Guided Meditation: Resting in Goodness
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a quiet guided meditation on resting in goodness rather than bracing against experience. He emphasizes letting ordinary goodness soothe agitation, forgiving the mind at a deep level, and trusting awareness and love to carry the practice. The talk gradually widens from settling posture to a softer relationship with thought, clinging, and whatever can be enjoyed in the present field of experience.
short_summary: A guided meditation on letting goodness soothe the heart, forgiving the mind, and relaxing into awareness.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: He welcomes the group and invites a simple settling into posture and presence.

chapters.title: Goodness as Soothe
chapters.description: The meditation begins by letting ordinary goodness calm and soften the heart.

chapters.title: Attention and Agitation
chapters.description: He contrasts the usual focus on breath and body with allowing goodness to ease worry and reveal what remains.

chapters.title: Pressure on Self
chapters.description: The talk turns toward how meditation can become a self-improvement project and how the self can be relaxed.

chapters.title: Meditation by Itself
chapters.description: He encourages surrendering the sense of self to awareness, then deep forgiveness of the mind and trust in awareness and love.

chapters.title: Clinging and Aliveness
chapters.description: He points to the goodness of mind as independent of effort, and explores how clinging narrows experience while non-clinging feels more alive.

chapters.title: Enjoy What Can Be
chapters.description: Near the end, he invites listeners to enjoy whatever is available in experience and let the practice rest in simple appreciation.

title: Guided Meditation: Grounding and Opening
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a grounded guided meditation centered on settling into the body, especially the belly, bones, and sense of earthiness. He repeatedly returns to the Buddha’s earth-touching gesture as a way to move attention out of thought and into direct bodily presence. As the practice unfolds, he opens into noticing affect and emotional activation with patience, then loosens ideas about how meditation or life should be, emphasizing simple aliveness over performance.
short_summary: A grounded guided meditation that uses the Buddha’s earth-touching gesture to settle attention into the body and open to present-moment experience.

chapters.title: Beginning Together
chapters.description: He invites the group to practice and settle into the meditation.

chapters.title: Posture and Belly
chapters.description: The practice begins with finding posture and bringing attention down into the belly, gravity, and the bones.

chapters.title: Touching the Earth
chapters.description: He uses the Buddha’s earth-touching gesture as a repeated image for grounding, asking listeners to know themselves as earth and breathe down into the ground.

chapters.title: Settling and Listening
chapters.description: After the initial grounding, the practice continues in a quieter register of resting, listening, and staying with bodily presence.

chapters.title: Affect in the Body
chapters.description: He turns toward emotional activation as bodily circuits that can be met with awareness and patience.

chapters.title: Dropping Expectations
chapters.description: The teaching shifts toward releasing hidden models of how life or meditation should unfold and using everything to become more alive.

chapters.title: Open Practice
chapters.description: The meditation continues in a broad, steady way, supporting simple presence without needing to control the experience.

chapters.title: No Performance
chapters.description: He closes by setting aside pleasure, preferences, and the pressure to perform.

title: Guided Meditation: Putting Down the World
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on what it means to “put down the world” without turning away from it. The practice moves from settling the body and mind, to resting without bartering for outcomes, to meeting experience directly through breath, sound, and sensation. In the final movement, he returns to the paradox that letting go can deepen love, care, and a more workable response to life.
short_summary: A guided meditation on putting down the world, resting in the present, and finding a fuller, more steady love.

chapters.title: Opening reflection
chapters.description: Brensilver opens with a brief reflection on practice as a radical surrender, putting the world down without abandoning care.

chapters.title: Settling the posture
chapters.description: The instruction turns to posture, alertness, brightness, and a non-tensed way of arriving.

chapters.title: Putting the world down
chapters.description: He names greed and distress with respect to the world, and explores the fear that letting go means neglecting what matters.

chapters.title: Autobiography and care
chapters.description: The talk deepens into the density of self-story, time, and compulsive care, pointing toward a deeper love beneath them.

chapters.title: Simple immediacy
chapters.description: Attention is guided toward the present-moment texture of sound, sensation, and breath, with less effort and less making.

chapters.title: No bartering
chapters.description: He cautions against using practice as a means to get somewhere, or as a buffer from intimacy with life.

chapters.title: Falling into the present
chapters.description: The meditation widens into resting without trading the moment away, and repeatedly returns to presence as something that can be entered rather than achieved.

chapters.title: Love after letting go
chapters.description: In the closing teaching, he returns to the idea that laying down the world can deepen love, reduce hatred, and make ordinary helpfulness more natural.

title: Guided Meditation: Breathing into Compassion
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with James Baldwin’s words on pain, connection, and the possibility of release through shared suffering. The practice then moves into sensing the body as a whole, settling attention, and using the breath to soften identification and open compassion outward. Near the end, he widens the frame to all beings and closes with a sense of how deeply lives intertwine.
short_summary: A guided meditation that uses the breath and body to turn pain toward connection and compassion.

chapters.title: Baldwin and pain
chapters.description: Matthew introduces James Baldwin’s reflection on suffering and frames personal pain as a way to connect with others.

chapters.title: Sense the body
chapters.description: The meditation shifts into directly knowing experience as sensation and settling into the body as the main field of practice.

chapters.title: Breath across the body
chapters.description: Attention follows the breath to see where it reaches, then opens to the whole body without strain.

chapters.title: Simple resting
chapters.description: He invites a relaxed, receptive posture of attention, with imagery and the inner gaze resting quietly as sensation unifies.

chapters.title: Pain as connection
chapters.description: The practice returns to suffering and reframes it as something that can connect us to others and depersonalize samsara.

chapters.title: Compassion opens wider
chapters.description: Compassion is encouraged to become broad and practical, without getting stuck in details or the sense that suffering is too vast.

chapters.title: Breath as love
chapters.description: The meditation deepens into a sense that breathing can convey love through the body and honor the shared condition of sentience.

chapters.title: Shared life and closing
chapters.description: He ends by pointing to how intertwined lives are and closes with a brief recitation and the sound of the bell.

title: Guided meditation
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation that begins with settling the body and noticing what feels pleasant in stillness. From there, he develops equanimity as a way to stop trying to rearrange experience, opening attention more broadly and softening the weight we give to thoughts, concerns, and even personal identity. The practice closes by emphasizing spaciousness, a lighter touch with the stream of experience, and the simple instruction to keep opening.
short_summary: A guided meditation on settling, equanimity, and opening attention with less urgency.

chapters.title: Settling the body
chapters.description: He invites listeners to find a posture and arrive in stillness in whatever position is available.

chapters.title: Finding what feels good
chapters.description: The guidance turns toward noticing small pleasantness in stopping and using that as a light anchor.

chapters.title: Less urgency, more equanimity
chapters.description: He reflects on worry, planning, and the ordinary pull of concerns, framing equanimity as staying with what is less urgent.

chapters.title: Breath as refuge
chapters.description: Attention narrows to the breath as something soothing and naturally regulating.

chapters.title: Mindfulness and letting go
chapters.description: He links mindfulness to setting aside greed and distress toward the world, and to turning inward without trying to fix the outside.

chapters.title: Offering the heart up
chapters.description: The meditation deepens into repeated reminders to meet the moment with trust and openness, without needing a particular outcome.

chapters.title: Widening attention
chapters.description: He invites a broader aperture of attention and explores how awareness can soften the heaviness objects and thoughts take on.

chapters.title: Lightness and space
chapters.description: The teaching returns to spaciousness, including a lighter relationship to autobiography and to attention moving without snagging.

chapters.title: Keep opening
chapters.description: The close offers a simple, repeated instruction to keep winding or opening, ending the practice gently.

title: Guided Meditation: Mostly Quiet Sit
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: This guided meditation begins with a brief welcome and then settles into a mostly quiet sit, with only occasional instruction. Matthew emphasizes sensing where attention feels appropriate, letting the Dharma become personal rather than merely borrowed from tradition. The sit moves through softening, support from sangha, and a spacious acceptance of present conditions, ending with a quieter closing bell sequence.
short_summary: A mostly quiet guided sit that moves from settling and softening into communal support and a deep acceptance of present conditions.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: After the welcome, the practice turns toward sitting and sensing where attention feels appropriate.

chapters.title: Personalizing Practice
chapters.description: Matthew frames the Dharma as something we learn from the Buddha and lineage, but also have to discover in our own life.

chapters.title: Letting Go
chapters.description: The guidance softens into a quiet release, with an image of the Buddha whispering to let go and not fear silence.

chapters.title: Sangha Support
chapters.description: The silent presence of the sangha is invited as a field of goodwill that can soothe the heart and make letting go feel safer.

chapters.title: Radical Permission
chapters.description: The meditation opens into a deeper acceptance of present conditions, alongside a reminder that wisdom later determines the balance between receptivity and action.

chapters.title: Closing Bells
chapters.description: The sit ends with a short sequence of bells and quiet closing space.

title: Guided Meditation: Self-acceptance
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver offers a guided meditation on self-acceptance that moves from settling the body into a broad, compassionate view of conditioning, history, and the forces that shape a life. He invites a radical yes to experience—not as a way to become better, but as the ground from which change can actually happen. The practice ends in a simple self-directed loving-kindness sequence: may I be happy, safe, protected, and at peace.
short_summary: A guided meditation that gently turns toward the body, conditioning, and a radical acceptance that opens into loving-kindness.

chapters.title: Settling In
chapters.description: The meditation begins by inviting stillness and an attentive arrival into practice.

chapters.title: Body and Awareness
chapters.description: Attention is poured into the body and the body is encouraged to feel bright and vivid with awareness.

chapters.title: Kindness for Conditioning
chapters.description: The teaching widens into compassion for the forces, seen and unseen, that have shaped this moment and this life.

chapters.title: Accepting My Life
chapters.description: He frames acceptance as a way of being with the whole of one's life, including suffering, good fortune, and helplessness.

chapters.title: A Radical Yes
chapters.description: The talk turns to the habit of sorting ourselves into acceptable and unacceptable parts and asks for a complete stop to that pattern.

chapters.title: End the Inner Game
chapters.description: Brensilver encourages dropping self-criticism, shame, and the constant behavioral correction project, and simply opening to the totality of what one is.

chapters.title: Loving-Kindness Closing
chapters.description: The practice closes with repeated phrases of goodwill: may I be happy, safe, protected, and at peace.

title: Guided meditation: Affect and the Lure of Thought
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a guided meditation on meeting affect directly rather than using thought to manage discomfort. He contrasts a gentle settling into practice with the more decisive attitude of “starting on a dime,” then repeatedly returns to renunciation as a willingness to feel what is present. The meditation emphasizes body-based attention, equanimity with emotional activation, and a quiet release of urgency and self-concern.
short_summary: A guided meditation on feeling emotional activation directly and loosening the habit of using thought for refuge.

chapters.title: Settling into practice
chapters.description: He begins by describing a gentle way of entering meditation and how the act of getting in can matter as much as where one arrives.

chapters.title: Start on a dime
chapters.description: Using Shinzen Young’s advice, he contrasts soft settling with a more immediate, decisive turn away from distraction.

chapters.title: Putting thoughts down
chapters.description: He names the sense of incompletion that can make thoughts feel urgent, and invites the listener to set them aside anyway.

chapters.title: Renunciation as feeling
chapters.description: Renunciation is framed not as suppression but as the willingness to feel the egoic wound, uncertainty, and ambiguity without using thought as a pacifier.

chapters.title: Body and emotion
chapters.description: The guidance shifts toward breathing, bodily awareness, and tending carefully to emotional circuits that insist thinking is necessary.

chapters.title: Equanimity and urgency
chapters.description: He explains how meeting emotional activation with equanimity can make everything feel less urgent.

chapters.title: This is your life
chapters.description: The talk turns toward presence in ordinary life, emphasizing that practice is not postponed until later and that this moment is already the whole field.

chapters.title: Release of self
chapters.description: He closes by pointing to a deepening intimacy with experience that requires loosening self-clinging and resting in what he calls the wondrous dark.

title: Guided Meditation: Attention and Love
speaker: Matthew Brensilver
podcast_description: Matthew Brensilver leads a quiet guided meditation that begins with settling the body, moving attention through chest, belly, breath, and earthiness. The practice gradually opens into a reflection on how attention reveals love and care, then turns toward the difficulty of hatred and the choice to meet it with breathing and affection. The close is sparse and intimate, with repeated pointers to simply feel love and allow it to be known.
short_summary: A guided meditation on grounding attention in the body and letting it reveal love, care, and a wiser response to hatred.

chapters.title: Opening and Settling
chapters.description: After a brief welcome, the talk moves into silence and the first instructions for settling into practice.

chapters.title: Arriving in the Body
chapters.description: Attention is invited to settle as if for the first time, preparing for a more embodied practice.

chapters.title: Chest, Breath, Belly
chapters.description: The meditation moves through the heart center, the lungs, and then down into the belly as a place of groundedness.

chapters.title: Earth and Rootedness
chapters.description: Breath is used to connect more deeply with the earth element and the steadiness of being rooted.

chapters.title: Attention Reveals Love
chapters.description: The teaching shifts from body awareness to the idea that careful attention helps reveal what we love and care about.

chapters.title: Patience with Longing
chapters.description: Rather than forcing answers, the practice emphasizes patience and letting the poignancy of being born arise on its own.
